## Summary

v2.3.0 turns the guided demo's spotlight into a real product-tour primitive — every step now aims it at its own target — and lands the first structural CSS refactor the v2.1.0 audit called for. Headline change is the spotlight system; the variant-tokens extraction is a quiet maintainability win that consumers won't notice but maintainers will.

## What's new

- **Multi-step demo spotlight** (`src/composables/useDemoSpotlight.ts`).
  The dim overlay used to be a single boolean (`fabFocused`) toggling a fixed radial gradient centred at 50%/50% of the viewport — it only worked for the FAB sequence because the FAB happened to live at the viewport centre. Now every demo step calls `spotlight.focus('.selector', { padding, soft })` to aim the overlay at its own element:

  | Step                  | Target                                      |
  | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
  | 1. Welcome            | `.hero` (intro framing)                     |
  | 2. Press play         | `.hero .mp` (the inline player)             |
  | 3. Container-aware    | `.resize-stage`                             |
  | 4. Drag-to-resize     | `.drag-stage`                               |
  | 5. Pick a mood        | `.variants` (re-aims during the descent)    |
  | 6. Floating FAB       | `.palette`                                  |
  | 7. Drag anywhere      | `.fab` (centre of viewport)                 |
  | 8. Vinyl & Aurora     | `.fab` (variant switch)                     |
  | 9. Pulso              | `.fab` + `.pulso-toggle` lifted via z-index |
  | 10. You're in         | `.hero` (closing reveal)                    |

  Four CSS variables drive the overlay geometry: `--spotlight-x`, `--spotlight-y`, `--spotlight-radius`, `--spotlight-soft`. Each is registered via `@property` (typed `<length>`), so the browser interpolates them at composite time — transitions between two demo targets are pure GPU work, no JS tween per frame.

  The composable observes `scroll` and `resize` (passive listeners), so the spotlight re-aims itself during scripted scrolls and window resizes without re-querying the DOM. `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` drops the interpolation and keeps a 200 ms opacity fade.

- **`src/lib/shared/variants.css`** — the four "mood" variant gradients (sunset, midnight, aurora, vinyl) used to live verbatim twice (once in `MusicPlayer.vue`, once in `MiniPlayer.vue`), with the matching accent RGB triplets repeated four times across the two files. They now live in a single shared module, exposed as CSS variables on the `[data-variant='X']` attribute:

  ```css
  [data-variant='sunset'] {
    --variant-bg-gradient: linear-gradient(135deg, …);
    --variant-accent-rgb: 245, 158, 11;
  }
  ```

  Both components reference `var(--variant-bg-gradient)` and `rgb(var(--variant-accent-rgb) / 0.18)` in their own scoped CSS. Each component still composes its own shadow stack on top — the FAB keeps its three-layer depth + ring + glow, the inline player keeps its single-layer accent inset — but the gradient and the accent identity are now in one place. Drift risk eliminated, adding a new mood is a two-line patch in the shared file plus one selector per consumer.

## What's fixed

- v2.1.0 audit P2 #8 (extract `shared/variants.css`) — closed.
- The spotlight gap the v2.1.0 audit didn't even surface — the demo's premium feel was held back by the single-target overlay.

## Breaking changes

None. The library API surface from v2.2.0 is preserved. The `fabFocused` ref is now a computed alias that proxies to the spotlight composable; the legacy `.fab-spotlight` CSS rules have been retired and replaced by `.demo-spotlight`.

## Quality gate

```
type-check   →  clean
lint         →  0 errors, 0 warnings (--max-warnings=0)
format       →  pass
tests        →  33 / 33
build        →  129 kB JS + 42 kB CSS → 48 kB gzip
build:lib    →  32 kB ES + 24 kB CJS + 25 kB CSS → ~14 kB gzip total
audit        →  0 vulnerabilities
npm pack     →  80 kB tarball, 24 files
```

## Files modified

```
A  src/composables/useDemoSpotlight.ts        (new — multi-step controller)
A  src/lib/shared/variants.css                (new — variant tokens)
A  .github/release-notes/v2.3.0.md           (this file)
M  src/lib/index.ts                           (side-effect import of variants.css)
M  src/lib/MusicPlayer.vue                    (variant rules use shared vars)
M  src/lib/MiniPlayer.vue                     (variant rules use shared vars)
M  src/App.vue                                (spotlight wiring on 10 steps + CSS overlay rewrite)
M  package.json                               (2.2.0 → 2.3.0)
```

## What's NOT in this release (deferred to v2.4.0)

- **Full FAB chrome shared partial** (audit P3 #14). The FAB chrome (cover stack, EQ bars, ring, noise filter) is duplicated between `MusicPlayer.vue`'s FAB-mode chrome and `MiniPlayer.vue`. The extraction is a 4-hour refactor with three regressions to check (centring, drag, pulso). Deferred to a focused v2.4.0 PR.
- **SVG noise filter `<defs>` extract** (audit P3 #15). Same reasoning.
- **Fab menu keyboard navigation Vitest tests** (audit P2 #11). The `<Teleport to="body">` + Pinia + jsdom combination in `@vue/test-utils` is brittle. Deferred until we add Cypress component testing.
